reasoning: Without search capabilities, we rely on the base rate. The National Heads-Up Poker Championship historically features a 64-player single-elimination bracket. The baseline probability of any average player winning is 1/64 (approx. 1.56%). Stephen Chidwick is one of the world's elite poker professionals, meaning his head-to-head win probability against an average opponent in this field is higher than 50% (likely 55-60%). Winning six consecutive heads-up matches at a 55% win rate yields a 2.7% chance of tournament victory; at a 60% win rate, it is about 4.6%. Given the high variance inherent in heads-up no-limit hold'em and the presence of other top professionals, an estimate of 3% is well-calibrated.
